algorithms.title: Game Theory: Nim & Grundy Numbers
algorithms.description: Game theory in competitive programming focuses on combinatorial games: two players alternate moves, last to move wins (normal play). Nim: heaps of stones, player removes any amount from one heap. Winning condition: XOR of all heap sizes != 0. Sprague-Grundy theorem: every combinatorial game is equivalent to a Nim heap of size equal to its Grundy number (mex of reachable states).
algorithms.intuition: Nim insight: if XOR of all heap sizes is 0, you're in a losing position (any move creates non-zero XOR for opponent). Key: XOR is the magic operation. Grundy number (nimber): for a position P, grundy(P) = mex({grundy(Q) : Q is reachable from P}). mex = minimum excludant = smallest non-negative integer not in the set. Two games combined: a player wins if XOR of Grundy numbers != 0.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Nim: compute XOR of all heap sizes. If XOR == 0: losing position (current player loses with optimal play). Else: winning position."},{"step":2,"description":"Winning move in Nim: find heap h where h XOR total_xor < h. Replace h with h XOR total_xor. This makes total XOR = 0."},{"step":3,"description":"Grundy: grundy(pos) = mex of grundy values of all positions reachable from pos. Compute bottom-up (terminal positions have grundy=0)."}]

algorithms.title: Reservoir Sampling
algorithms.description: Reservoir sampling selects K items uniformly at random from a stream of N items where N is unknown or too large to store. Each item in the stream has exactly K/N probability of being selected. Algorithm R by Vitter: maintain a reservoir of K items, replace reservoir[j] with item i with probability K/i for each new item i.
algorithms.intuition: For K=1: process items 1,2,...n. Keep item 1. For item i: replace with probability 1/i. After N items, each has been kept with probability 1/N (equal chance). Proof: item i is selected at step i with prob 1/i. It's not replaced by item i+1 with prob i/(i+1). Surviving to the end: 1/i × i/(i+1) × (i+1)/(i+2) × ... × (n-1)/n = 1/n. For K>1: replace reservoir[j] (random index) with new item with probability K/i.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Fill reservoir with first K items."},{"step":2,"description":"For each subsequent item i (from K+1 to N):"},{"step":3,"description":"Generate random j in [0, i)."},{"step":4,"description":"If j < K: replace reservoir[j] with item i."}]
